game_name: Battletoads & Double Dragon: The Ultimate Team

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all subsequent combat scenarios
Platform traversal with fall risk; must learn finger-timing mechanic
Backward kick attacks while moving; different tempo than foot combat

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from start (Kiss-My-Fist, Big Bad Foot, Nuclear Knuckles, etc.)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Power attacks essential for boss damage; discovered through play

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from start (spin kick, knee drops, elbow drops)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dragon Force techniques; alternative moveset to toads
Genre shift from beat-em-up to shooter; asteroids, mines, UFOs
New platforming mechanic with instant fail state (finger stepping)
Final gauntlet; no continues available after initial three
Transition level between shmup and final boss sequence

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to grounded combat after platform sections
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating intensity: grounded beat-em-up → platforming hazards → genre shift to shmup → boss gauntlet with no continues; rhythm driven by environmental hazards and mechanic introductions rather than time-based cycles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to basic melee and rope mechanics; enemies introduced gradually; Colossus tail environment is relatively open
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased platform complexity with rope hazards; fall damage risk escalates; speeder bike sequences introduce new tempo; interior ship corridors tighten navigation space
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dramatic genre pivot to shmup (Level 4) creates learning curve spike; precision platforming with finger-stepping mechanic; consecutive boss fights (Levels 6-7) with no continues; Dark Queen final battle concentrates all learned mechanics
notable_spikes: Level 4 genre shift from beat-em-up to shooter (asteroids, mines, UFOs)
Finger-stepping platforming mechanic introduction (instant death fall state)
Final boss sequence (Levels 6-7) with only 3 continues for entire game

notable_level_patterns.description: Linear progression through enemy waves; horizontal scrolling with open ground and elevated platform sections; primary use of melee attacks and basic combos
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 (Colossus tail), Level 2 (interior corridors initial sections)

notable_level_patterns.description: Rope/cable hanging sequences with fall damage risk; requires precise timing and navigation across elevated sections; introduces multi-mechanic choreography (combat + traversal)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 2 (ship interior), Level 3 (ship base upper sections)

notable_level_patterns.description: Combines speeder bike sections with grounded combat; different attack patterns required (backward kicks while mounted); pacing accelerates compared to foot-based levels
Complete departure from beat-em-up; overhead/side-scrolling spaceship shooter; dodge-focused rather than attack-focused; introduces resource management (ship health)
Two consecutive single-opponent boss encounters with escalating complexity; no mid-level enemy encounters; mechanical memorization and pattern recognition required
